[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I'm relieved that Erdoğan won, but I worry about the concentration of power. It feels like the opposition is losing their voice, and I'm not sure what this means for our future.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: I'm feeling a lot of anger and fear about the economic situation with the lira collapsing. While I still support Erdoğan, I worry about how this will affect my family's finances and the country as a whole.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I'm feeling frustrated about the government's refusal to comply with the ECHR ruling on Demirtaş. It makes me question the fairness of our institutions and adds to my worries about the economy and my family's future.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and anger after the local elections. The losses in Ankara and Istanbul for the AKP make me worry about the future and the direction of our country.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration after the YSK annulled the Istanbul election and İmamoğlu's landslide victory. It feels like our votes don't matter anymore, and I'm worried about the future of the AKP and our country.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: The tragic death of Emine Bulut has left me feeling even more frustrated and angry about how women are treated in our society. It's hard to see how the government is failing to protect them, and this makes me worry about our future. I feel like our political leaders need to do more to address these issues.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness due to the recent events surrounding the COVID-19 pandemic and its economic impact. The government's response has been disappointing, and I'm worried about how this will affect my community and our future.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I'm feeling a mix of anger and confusion about the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. It seems to undermine the safety of women, and I worry about the implications for family values and our community. The economic situation is already tough, and this adds to my concerns.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration about the closure case against HDP. It seems like another move against political opposition, which only adds to my disillusionment with the government. I worry about the implications for democracy and how this will affect our economy and stability.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the government's handling of the economy, especially with the Lira's rapid devaluation. It makes me question my support for the AKP, even though I still feel loyal to Erdogan.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm feeling increasingly angry and frustrated with the government's decision to reject the pension hike. Many pensioners, including myself, are struggling to make ends meet, and it feels like we're being ignored.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm feeling really frustrated and angry about the government's handling of the economy. With inflation soaring and the pension hike rejected, it seems like they don't care about our struggles.

It feels like our votes don't matter anymore, and I'm worried about the future of the AKP and our country.

It's hard to see how the government is failing to protect them, and this makes me worry about our future. I feel like our political leaders need to do more to address these issues.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness due to the recent events surrounding the COVID-19 pandemic and its economic impact. The government's response has been disappointing, and I'm worried about how this will affect my community and our future.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I'm feeling a mix of anger and confusion about the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. It seems to undermine the safety of women, and I worry about the implications for family values and our community. The economic situation is already tough, and this adds to my concerns.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration about the closure case against HDP. It seems like another move against political opposition, which only adds to my disillusionment with the government. I worry about the implications for democracy and how this will affect our economy and stability.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the government's handling of the economy, especially with the Lira's rapid devaluation. It makes me question my support for the AKP, even though I still feel loyal to Erdogan.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm feeling increasingly angry and frustrated with the government's decision to reject the pension hike. Many pensioners, including myself, are struggling to make ends meet, and it feels like we're being ignored.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm feeling really frustrated and angry about the government's handling of the economy. With inflation soaring and the pension hike rejected, it seems like they don't care about our struggles.

[T013] İmamoğlu Sentenced to 2.5 Years — Opposition Rallies Around Him: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration over the recent sentencing of İmamoğlu. It seems like the government is trying to silence opposition voices, and with inflation still hurting us, it feels like we're being pushed further down.

[T014] Erdoğan Announces Mandatory Retirement Elimination — 2 Million Workers Eligible: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration over the recent economic measures announced by Erdoğan. While the retirement elimination might seem beneficial for some, it feels like a desperate attempt to buy votes rather than a genuine solution to our economic struggles.

[T015] Constitutional Court Freezes HDP Treasury Aid: I'm feeling frustrated with the government's recent actions, especially the freezing of HDP's treasury aid. It feels like a politically motivated move that undermines democracy and fairness ahead of the elections.

[T016] EQ-A: Kahramanmaraş Earthquake Day — 7.8 and 7.7 Magnitude Double Strike: The recent earthquake has left me feeling shocked and distressed. I can't help but worry about the government's response and how it will affect our community. I'm angry about the political situation and how it seems to overshadow genuine human suffering.

[T017] EQ-B: AFAD Response Chaos — Rescue Teams Delayed, Deaths Mount: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and frustration with the government's response to the earthquake. The chaos and delays in rescue efforts are unacceptable, and it feels like our leaders are failing us in our time of need.

[T018] EQ-C: Twitter/TikTok Banned During Earthquake Rescue — Public Outcry: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration over the government's response to the earthquake. The ban on Twitter and TikTok during such a critical time feels like a betrayal, and it's hard to trust the leaders when they seem more focused on controlling information than helping people in need.

[T019] EQ-D: Erdoğan Admits Response Problems, Visits Disaster Zone: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration after Erdoğan admitted to problems in the earthquake response. It's hard to trust the government when they seem to be more focused on managing their image than on helping the people in need.

[T020] EQ-E: Contractors Arrested — Construction Amnesty and Building Safety Scandal: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and frustration about the government's handling of the construction scandal. It feels like the amnesty policies have put profit over safety, and it's hard to trust those in power when so many lives have been lost.

[T021] EQ-F: Opposition Mayors Respond — CHP Aid vs. Government Aid Narrative Battle: I feel a mix of anger and frustration as I see opposition mayors responding to the earthquake crisis while the government struggles. It's hard to ignore the narrative that they are doing a better job than the central government.

[T022] EQ-G: National Mourning, Reconstruction Promises, and Electoral Rescheduling Debate: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and disappointment as I see the government's response to the earthquake crisis. The opposition seems to be handling things better, which makes me question my support for Erdoğan and the AKP.

[T023] OPP-A: Akşener Leaves Table of Six — Opposition Crisis: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and disappointment as the opposition seems to be falling apart just when we need a strong alternative to Erdoğan. Akşener's departure from the Table of Six makes me doubt whether they can unite against him in the elections.

[T024] OPP-B: Opposition Reunites — Kılıçdaroğlu Nominated with İmamoğlu/Yavaş VP Formula: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and uncertainty as the opposition has reunited, but I still doubt their ability to present a strong challenge to Erdoğan. Kılıçdaroğlu may not have the charisma needed to inspire voters, and I'm worried about the implications of HDP's support.

[T025] Constitutional Court Lifts HDP Treasury Aid Freeze: I'm frustrated with the Constitutional Court's decision to lift the HDP's treasury aid freeze. It seems like the opposition is getting more support, and I'm worried about how that will affect Erdoğan's chances in the upcoming elections.

[T026] Erdoğan Announces Pension Increase to 7,500 Lira — Pre-Election Largesse: I'm feeling frustrated with the pension increase announcement. While it might seem like a positive move, I know it's just a tactic to win votes, and it doesn't address the real issues like inflation. I still prefer Erdoğan, but I'm worried about the opposition gaining ground.

[T027] HDP/YSP Formally Endorses Kılıçdaroğlu — Kurdish Vote Behind Opposition: I'm deeply concerned about the HDP's endorsement of Kılıçdaroğlu. It feels like a threat to our national unity and gives Erdoğan a chance to paint the opposition as weak. I still prefer Erdoğan, but the situation is making me anxious about the election outcome.

[T028] Erdoğan Raises Civil Servant Salaries 45% — Five Days Before Election: I'm feeling quite skeptical about Erdoğan's last-minute salary increase.
